description: Document structure template for engage-prism analysis proposal generation (Step 5). Slim by design — decision-focused; raw subagent envelopes live in the sibling PRISM-{question-slug}.audit.json file. Target: main proposal < 8KB for typical runs.
sizeTarget: Main proposal < 8KB for typical runs. Keep decision-focused; offload raw structured data to the audit JSON sibling file.

sections.heading: > ⚠️ **Evidence-fabrication risk detected** on one or more paths.
sections.content: Banner is rendered ONLY when the synthesis output sets fabricationRisk=true (see #220). Renders at the very top of the document, above the metadata heading. Names the affected path(s) and states: 'A subagent on this path returned zero-fetch evidence twice (performed=false with non-conforming attemptedCalls[], or performed=true with fetchCount=0) and was tagged evidence-fabrication-risk — distinct from environmental degradation. Affected path's citations did not survive primary-agent verification; treat its claims as unverified.'

fields.label: Evidence-Fabrication Risk
fields.description: Set true (#220) when one or more paths returned zero-fetch evidence twice and was tagged evidence-fabrication-risk by return-side validation. Distinct from environmental degradation; surfaces the fabrication-risk-banner section at the top of the proposal.

sections.heading: ## Research Plan
sections.content: Entity anchors, source classes, recency window, authority preferences, and fetch budget from Step 0. If webResearchPerformed=false, this section includes the degradation reason and the implication for the recommendation.

sections.heading: ## Signal Analysis
sections.content: One-line footnote only: name the matched signals or 'adaptive mode (no signal match)' or 'default routing — primary agent named paths directly'. Full signal-matching detail (weights, per-paradigm scores, rejected angles) lives in the sibling PRISM-{question-slug}.audit.json under signalAnalysis[]. Do not repeat the signal table inline — the main proposal is decision-focused.

subsections.heading: ### Brief
subsections.content: One-sentence angle summary plus the path's declared primarySourceClass. Full brief (researchPlan, exclusions, constraints) lives in the audit JSON sibling file under paths[N].brief.

subsections.heading: ### Report
subsections.content: Narrative summary only (3–6 sentences): core claim, strongest citation (title + one-line excerpt), notable gaps. Full structured report — citations[], webResearch, attemptedCalls[], evidenceBase, analysis steps, recommendation — lives in the audit JSON sibling file under paths[N].report. Never paste raw JSON inline.

subsections.heading: ### Disagreement Audit
subsections.content: Convergent flag (true|false) and named disagreement points when convergent=false. On convergent=true, include the consensus-or-groupthink caveat. See disagreement-audit phase in synthesis-config.json.

subsections.heading: ### Bear Path Outcome
subsections.content: When bearPathTriggered=true, state whether the bear survived validation and the one-line counter-case summary. When bearPathTriggered=false, omit this subsection.

subsections.heading: ### Scoring Summary
subsections.content: One-line-per-path winner + disqualification notes only. Full scoring matrix (per-dimension scores) lives in the audit JSON sibling under synthesis.scoringMatrix.

subsections.heading: ### Hybridization
subsections.content: Named hybrid (if any) with specific integration point. Skip if no hybrid.

sections.heading: ## Recommendation
sections.content: Final recommendation with rationale that references specific citations. Must explicitly surface convergent flag, bear-path outcome, and any degraded path.

sections.heading: ## What Would Change This Answer
sections.content: 2-3 specific observations — new evidence, updated input, or counterfactual — that would flip the recommendation.

sections.heading: ## Audit
sections.content: Raw subagent envelopes, full citation lists, per-dimension scoring matrix, attemptedCalls[] records, and signal-matching tables are written alongside this proposal to PRISM-{question-slug}.audit.json. The audit file is machine-readable JSON conforming to the report-schema.json shape for each path plus a synthesis block. Keep the main proposal decision-focused; consult the audit JSON for verification.
